category_title: Autistic students with correct mathematical processing and matching gestures
student_description: Awal is an autistic student who successfully processes abstract mathematical concepts, like geometry, when they are firmly grounded in concrete, everyday objects. He relies heavily on explicit visual and gestural cues, specifically needing the teacher to point to or directly highlight the images he needs to choose between. Without these explicit directional cues and concrete references, he tends to freeze and struggle to visually distinguish the shapes.

criteria.rationale: Awal processes mathematical concepts much more easily when his learning environment is consistently supported by visual and physical media.
criteria.check: The long-term plan must include the consistent use of physical or visual figurative media (e.g., toys, blocks, posters, images) as core learning tools.

criteria.rationale: Starting with abstract concepts causes Awal to stall; he requires a foundational understanding built on tangible reality before abstracting.
criteria.check: The long-term plan must explicitly structure lessons to introduce new geometric forms using everyday concrete objects (e.g., dice, cardboard) before transitioning to their abstract geometric names.
